title: Parameters Shared Across Time
concept: An RNN uses the exact same weights at every time step, letting it detect patterns regardless of where they appear in the sequence.

exercises.prompt: What does 'sharing parameters across time' mean in an RNN?

options.text: Using the same weights at every step
Using different weights for every word
Deleting the weights after one use

exercises.explain: By reusing the same weights at each step, RNNs can handle inputs of any length and recognize patterns no matter where they appear in a sequence.

cards.title: Recycle Your Brainpower!
cards.body: Imagine if you had to relearn the meaning of the word 'apple' depending on whether it was the first, fifth, or tenth word in a book. That would be exhausting! Thankfully, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) share their memory rules across time to avoid this exact problem.

cards.title: What are Shared Parameters?
cards.body: In an RNN, 'shared parameters' means we use the exact same mathematical weights (multipliers) to process the input at time step 1, time step 2, and every step after. Instead of learning new rules for every position in a sentence, the network uses the same rule everywhere.

cards.title: The Sliding Lens
cards.body: Think of an RNN like a magnifying glass. As you slide it across a sentence word by word, the glass doesn't change shape or power. It uses the exact same lens to examine every single word, no matter where it is located.

cards.title: Spotting Words Anywhere
cards.body: Imagine detecting the word 'urgent' in an email. Whether 'urgent' appears at the very beginning ('Urgent: please reply') or at the end ('This is extremely urgent'), the RNN uses the exact same weights to detect it. It doesn't need to relearn what 'urgent' means at different positions!

cards.title: Changing Data, Constant Rules
cards.body: Some people think that because an RNN's inputs and hidden states change at every step, its weights must change too. That's a myth! The data changes as it flows through, but the weights (the network's internal rules) remain identical across all time steps.

cards.title: Shared vs. Unique Weights
cards.body: Let's compare a network that shares weights across time with one that does not.

left.text: Uses weight set 'W' at step 1, step 2, and step 3. The rules stay constant.

right.text: Uses weight 'W1' at step 1, 'W2' at step 2, and 'W3' at step 3. The model size grows.

exercises.prompt: What is a major benefit of sharing parameters across time in an RNN?

options.text: It allows the model to find patterns regardless of where they appear
It makes the model learn a new set of weights for every single word
It prevents the model from processing sequences longer than 5 words
It forces the model to ignore all inputs after the first step

exercises.explain: Sharing weights across time means the network has far fewer parameters to learn, and it can process sequences of any length using the same rules.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An RNN changes its weight values to new set of numbers at every new time step.
exercises.explain: The weights (parameters) in an RNN remain the same at every time step. It is the inputs and the hidden states that change.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the rule about RNN weight sharing:
exercises.sentence: An RNN uses the exact _____ weights at every time step.
exercises.explain: An RNN shares its weights across all time steps. Using the same weights allows it to find patterns anywhere in the sequence without needing a massive model.

exercises.prompt: Two AI models are discussing how they process a 3-word sentence. Which one is behaving like a standard RNN?

options.text: AI Model A: 'I will reuse the same weight set at step 1, step 2, and step 3.'
AI Model B: 'I need to create new, independent weights for every time step.'
AI Model C: 'I only use weights at step 1 and discard them for step 2.'

exercises.explain: AI Model A is correct because RNNs reuse the same weights at every step. AI Model B incorrectly suggests that new weights are created for each step, which would make the model balloon in size.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes how Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) manage their weights across a sequence?

options.text: They use different weights for each word to remember them better
They reuse the exact same weight matrices at every time step
They reset all weights to zero after reading three words
They only use weights at the very first and last words

exercises.explain: By using the exact same weight matrix (U, W, and V) at every single time step, RNNs keep their parameter count constant and can process input sequences of any length. If they used different weights for each step, they would need a different model size for every sentence length!
